section: Chapter 2: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What is an electric current?
questions.correct_answer: The flow of electric charges through a conductor
questions.explanation: Electric current flows in a closed circuit when a source such as a battery is connected.

questions.question: What is an electric circuit?
questions.correct_answer: A closed path through which electric current flows
questions.explanation: A circuit usually contains a battery, wires, a switch, and an electrical device such as a bulb.

questions.question: What is meant by a closed circuit?
questions.correct_answer: A circuit in which the path for current flow is complete
questions.explanation: When the switch is closed, the circuit becomes complete and current flows.

questions.question: What is the heating effect of electric current?
questions.correct_answer: The production of heat when electric current flows through a conductor
questions.explanation: Electric heaters, irons, and toasters use the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: What is a fuse?
questions.correct_answer: A safety device that protects circuits by melting when current is too high
questions.explanation: A fuse wire melts and breaks the circuit when excessive current flows.

questions.question: What is the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.correct_answer: The phenomenon where a current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field
questions.explanation: When current passes through a wire, it behaves like a magnet.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?
questions.correct_answer: A magnet created by passing electric current through a coil of wire around an iron core
questions.explanation: Electromagnets are widely used in electric bells, cranes, and motors.

questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
questions.correct_answer: By increasing the number of coil turns or increasing the electric current
questions.explanation: More coil turns or stronger current produces a stronger magnetic field.

questions.question: Name one device that uses electromagnets.
questions.explanation: Electric bells use electromagnets to move a striker that hits the bell repeatedly.

questions.question: What happens if a circuit has a break?
questions.explanation: An open circuit prevents the flow of current and the device connected will not work.

notes.title: Electric Current
notes.content: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor in a closed circuit.

notes.title: Electric Circuit
notes.content: An electric circuit is a complete path that allows electric current to flow from a power source through components and back to the source.

notes.title: Heating Effect of Current
notes.content: When electric current flows through a conductor, heat is produced due to resistance.

notes.title: Magnetic Effect of Current
notes.content: A current-carrying conductor creates a magnetic field around it.

notes.title: Electromagnets
notes.content: Electromagnets are temporary magnets created by electric current and can be turned on or off.

facts.fact: Electric current flows only in a closed circuit.
Devices like electric irons and heaters use the heating effect of current.
An electromagnet loses its magnetism when electric current stops.
Electromagnets are used in cranes to lift heavy iron objects.
